topic: Sharp non-equivalence between Athey-Imbens 2006 changes-in-changes and Callaway-Li 2019 group-time quantile estimators for the QTT under staggered adoption with heterogeneous treatment-timing distributions, exhibiting a concrete moment of the timing distribution at which the two estimators have opposite asymptotic sign and recovering joint point identification of the QTT under timing homogeneity

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the sharp finite-support frontier where Athey-Imbens CIC and Callaway-Li group-time QTT agree under timing homogeneity but have opposite asymptotic sign under a specified heterogeneous timing-distribution moment.

source_refs.claim: CIC identifies the treated group's full counterfactual distribution using a nonlinear DID model with stable ranks and scale-invariant structure.
source_refs.author: Athey and Imbens

source_refs.claim: Panel-data QTT is identified by combining distributional DID with a time-invariance copula restriction.
source_refs.author: Callaway and Li

source_refs.claim: The staggered-adoption QTT paper points out challenges in generalizing the Callaway-Li time-invariance copula to the staggered setup.
source_refs.author: Li and Lin

source_refs.excerpt: claim (ii) equals the usual plug-in quantile-difference formula

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published anchors identify counterfactual distributions under different rank and copula restrictions, and Li-Lin explicitly leaves the staggered Callaway-Li copula generalization delicate; the prior CausalSmith attempt only had a taxonomy and a threshold diagnostic, with reviewers noting that the Athey-Imbens and Callaway-Li assumptions were cited without a computable equality or separation test. The concrete obstacle is nonlinear mixing: cohort timing changes enter before quantile inversion, so a timing-distribution moment can change signs even when the primitive group-time distributional changes look aligned.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient equality theorem for the two counterfactual CDFs at all continuity points, plus a finite-support counterexample indexed by a timing-distribution moment and a timing-homogeneity corollary giving joint point identification of QTT.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify the unique coarsest finite-tail quotient and exact active rank needed to certify threshold-coded CQTE claims under marginal sensitivity.

source_refs.excerpt: unique coarsest quotient with this property

open_problems.why_unsolved: The reviewer evidence says scalar QTE envelopes and breakdown frontiers were too close to prior art; the remaining gap is the claim-specific finite quotient/rank object, and it only becomes publishable if it yields a simultaneous-inference or linear-program complexity payoff not implied by coordinatewise envelopes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A minimal finite-tail certificate theorem proving quotient uniqueness, lower bounds on active LP dimension, and a simultaneous-inference or audit-complexity improvement for threshold-coded CQTE claims.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove sharpness of the bounded-anticipation support interval for staggered DID aggregates when the same anticipation coordinate enters multiple cohort-period contrasts with opposite signs.

source_refs.excerpt: the interval in Theorem is the sharp identified set
support interval may be conservative rather than sharp

open_problems.why_unsolved: The proposal flags that pooled signed coefficients may overstate the attainable interval once the same anticipation distortion is shared across cohort-period contrasts; the missing piece is a variation-independent witness construction or, if that construction fails, a tighter support function over coupled anticipation coordinates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp support-function theorem for ATT(g,t) and finite staggered-DID aggregates, including a potential-outcome witness construction showing when pooled signed coefficients are necessary and sufficient.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp bounds for staggered-adoption group-time QTT when neither group-invariance nor time-invariance copula restrictions can be maintained.

source_refs.claim: Sharp bounds are available for distributional and quantile DID in a simpler two-period setting under a direct extension of mean DID assumptions.
source_refs.author: Fan and Yu

source_refs.claim: Two-period CQTT identification uses conditional distributional DID plus a copula restriction linking untreated changes and baseline untreated outcomes.
source_refs.author: Callaway, Li, and Oka

source_refs.claim: Staggered QTT identification is shown under distributional DID and group-invariance copula assumptions, while the time-invariance copula route is flagged as challenging.
source_refs.author: Li and Lin

open_problems.why_unsolved: The existing partial-identification anchor is essentially two-period, while the staggered point-ID literature imposes copula restrictions to recover group-time QTT. Without those restrictions, cohort-calendar mixtures and changing risk sets create feasible latent rank processes that are not summarized by the observed marginal distributions used in two-period bounds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Sharp Frechet-Hoeffding/Makarov-style bounds for each QTT(g,t,tau) and for cohort aggregates, with a proof that every bound point is attained by a latent rank process consistent with the observed staggered panel marginals.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop a Goodman-Bacon or Sun-Abraham style decomposition for distributional and quantile DID estimators under staggered timing.

source_refs.claim: TWFE DID with timing variation decomposes into weighted two-group/two-period DID comparisons.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.claim: Event-study lead and lag coefficients can be contaminated by effects from other periods under heterogeneous treatment effects.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: TWFE estimators can be misleading with heterogeneous treatment effects, motivating heterogeneity-robust DID alternatives.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille

open_problems.why_unsolved: The known staggered-DID decompositions are linear mean functionals, whereas CIC and QTT estimators are nonlinear maps from cohort-time distributions through counterfactual CDF construction and quantile inversion. A direct negative-weight decomposition is therefore absent; the obstacle is finding the correct influence-function or directional-derivative object whose components expose sign reversal and contamination.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A Gateaux-linearized decomposition theorem for quantile/CIC estimators into cohort-time influence-function terms, plus a finite-sample diagnostic identifying sign-reversal and contamination components.

open_problems.open_problem: Classify which outcome transformations preserve the sign and ordering of staggered-adoption CIC, distributional DID, and Callaway-Li QTT estimands.

source_refs.claim: Mean DID parallel-trends conclusions can be sensitive to the functional form chosen for the analysis.
source_refs.author: Roth and Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: CIC is motivated partly by nonlinear DID assumptions that are invariant to scaling of the outcome.
source_refs.author: Athey and Imbens

source_refs.claim: Distribution-regression DID work relates distributional DID assumptions to changes-in-changes-style counterfactual recovery.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Fernandez-Val, and Luo

open_problems.why_unsolved: Functional-form sensitivity is characterized for mean DID, and CIC has its own scale-invariance motivation, but staggered distributional estimands combine transformation, cohort mixture, CDF inversion, and quantile subtraction. The unresolved issue is whether monotone transformations preserve only rank-level statements or also preserve sign and ordering of QTT contrasts after timing aggregation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A transformation-class theorem giving necessary and sufficient conditions on h for CIC, distributional DID, and Callaway-Li QTT signs or orderings to be invariant, with counterexamples where the transformation changes the sign.

literature_map:
Athey and Imbens (2006, Econometrica) is the CIC anchor: nonlinear DID, scale invariance, and full counterfactual distributions.
Fan and Yu (2012, Economics Letters) is the partial-identification anchor for distributional and quantile DID.
Callaway, Li, and Oka (2018, Journal of Econometrics) identifies CQTT with two periods using distributional DID plus a group-invariance copula.
Callaway and Li (2019, Quantitative Economics) identifies panel-data QTT using distributional DID plus time-invariance copula stability.
Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics) supplies the group-time ATT architecture for staggered adoption.
Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics), Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics), and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) show how staggered timing and heterogeneity contaminate linear mean DID/TWFE estimands.
Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, Review of Economic Studies) gives efficient imputation logic for staggered mean event studies, suggesting an efficiency benchmark missing for QTT.
Li and Lin (2024, Economics Letters) directly generalizes a quantile DID route to staggered adoption and explicitly flags the Callaway-Li copula extension difficulty.
Kim and Wooldridge (2025, JBES) and Miller-Galvao (2026, SSRN) add recent QTT DID estimators, including doubly robust staggered QTT.
Ciaccio (2025, arXiv) studies distributional DID with multiple periods and staggered adoption, emphasizing recovery of entire counterfactual distributions.
Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica) is the functional-form sensitivity anchor for DID assumptions, not yet fully translated to staggered QTT/CIC.
Imbens and Angrist (1994, Econometrica), de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2018, ReStud), and Manski-Tamer (2002, Econometrica) are adjacent anchors for LATE, fuzzy DID, and interval-data partial identification gaps.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Target qid has only init state/pipeline in active research; no proposal text was available under its folder.
2. Closest quantile/QTT evidence is eid_dist_synth_control_v1, especially Conjecture 2 and angle2 reviews citing Athey-Imbens and Callaway-Li.
3. Strongest staggered-DID partial-ID evidence is pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: bounded anticipation, pooled signed coefficients, zero-bound recovery.
4. Continuous-DID ACR support-gap material in eid_continuous_did_acr_panel_v1 is adjacent but derivative/ACR rather than QTT.
5. q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 supplies a named-estimator DID non-equivalence frontier with bad controls.
6. panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 supplies panel estimator non-equivalence frontier evidence from proposal, rejected angle, and bank README.
7. pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 supplies quantile partial-ID/certificate gaps; reviewer strengthening_paths requested a simultaneous-inference or LP-complexity theorem.
